summary: This page introduces the classification of signals and lists five major categories considered in the book: continuous-time/discrete-time, analog/digital, periodic/aperiodic, energy/power, and deterministic/probabilistic. It then begins subsection 1.2-1 by defining continuous-time signals as those specified for every time value and discrete-time signals as those specified only at discrete time instants, with Figure 1.4 illustrating both types.
